title: C++ evolution updates for Glassbox
summary:
I spent the last few days working on the C++ side of Glassbox. The evolution loop was getting stuck in local minima, so I added staged evolution and adaptive restarts. The model now detects if it is stagnant and triggers a mix of mutated elites and random immigrants. This helps it find better expressions.

I also updated the reliability scoring. Now it keeps track of both raw MSE and display-formula MSE. If the evaluator fails or gets non-finite values, it handles them properly instead of crashing. I added native JSONL trace logging so I can debug the generations easier. Everything is exposed through pybind so I can tune the arithmetic temperature from Python. Ran the regression tests and they passed.
commitMessages: feat: enhance README with new features including reliability scoring, C++ tracing, and evolution controls
feat(sr-cpp): added staged evolution and adaptive restart controls
feat(sr): harden C++ evolution reliability and add native trace logging
